TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Sakizaya, story: skzyNr-tribalhistory_sinay

S.FORM: hang malingatu kaku i mahini musakamu tu pakainiay i i tawya ay henay nuyni u babalaki.
S.TRANSL: 我現在要說有關祖先的事。
I am going to talk about the ancestors.

S.FORM: nu ayawan nu cacay alasubu izaw ku tulu a bataan a mihcaan na i tiniay i Takubuanan kiza kuyni u babalaki.
S.TRANSL: 一百三十年前祖先在達固部岸部落。
One hundred and thirty years ago, the ancestors were in Takubuan tribe.

S.FORM: saka maliwasak kuyni u niyazu’ na i tizaay i Takubuan.
S.TRANSL: 所以達固部岸部落就分散了。
Therefore, Tkubuan tribe scattered.

S.FORM: uyni han tu u babalaki nu niyam kya hican.
S.TRANSL: 至於我們的祖先們該如何?
How were our ancestors?

S.FORM: u namu han tu a balucu’ mikilim tuyni u kauzipan u kakalukan i sa kuyni ci Kumud Pazik u tumuk nu Takubuan.
S.TRANSL: 達固部岸的頭目Kumud Pazik 說你們去尋找生存的地方與耕地。
Kumud Pazik, the leader of Takubuan tribe, said that you went to find the places for living and farming.

S.FORM: saka maini tini i wali kuyni u babalaki milakec tuyni u alangahay i tini i hualiandachiau.
S.TRANSL: 祖先從東邊涉水過河（現今的花蓮大橋）。
The ancestors waded the river, Hualian bridge, from the east.

S.FORM: maini i wali tatimul sa katukuh i tini i Kaluwa kya.
S.TRANSL: 往南抵達Kaluwa。
(They) went south to Kaluwa.

S.FORM: E pinaay ku demiad hakya u labi i hakya mimelaw tuyni u kaka kakaliumahan i tiza kakalukan.
S.TRANSL: 不知多少日夜尋找耕地。
(It took them) numerous days and nights to look for the farmland.

S.FORM: a u canan kuyu sakauzip sisa.
S.TRANSL: 要如何生活呢?
How do they make a living?

S.FORM: i tatenga’ mimelawmelaw kuyni u babalaki sa.
S.TRANSL: 祖先到處看。
The ancestors looked around.

S.FORM: ca’ay i katatungus i tini i Kaluwa.
S.TRANSL: Kaluwa 不適合（生活）。
Kaluwa was not suitable (for life).

S.FORM: isa nayay kuyza u nanum cay kaitaneng.
S.TRANSL: 因為水不夠。
Because water was not enough.

S.FORM: E tayni tu i timul tayni i timul sa.
S.TRANSL: 往南走。
(They) went south.

S.FORM: i tatenga’ situl sa i tini i i tini i Maibul.
S.TRANSL: 走到馬立雲。
(They) arrived at Maibul.

S.FORM: nayi’ henay ku zazan tayni i hiya i timul.
S.TRANSL: 往南邊還沒有路。
There was not way to the south.

S.FORM: uyu kakaliumahan sa a tacuwaan tu i tini i hiya i Sapad.
S.TRANSL: 耕地是在掃把頂。
The farmland was in Sapad.

S.FORM: ahbal kyuni u kakaliumahan.
S.TRANSL: 耕地廣闊。
The farmland was wide.

S.FORM: u palumaan nu Maibul i tawya nu babalaki sa i tini i wali.
S.TRANSL: 那時候馬立雲的住戶是住在（馬立雲的）東邊。
The residents of Maibul were (living in) the eastside (of Maibul).

S.FORM: i tini i zazan nu silamalay anu hiya nu cidusya nayi’ay henay kuyza u zazan nu silamalay i tawya.
S.TRANSL: 在鐵路或公路上當時還沒有鐵軌。
At that time, there was no tracks on railroad or road.

S.FORM: yu mahiza sa i mapulung i wali kuyni u luma nuyu babalaki nu Maibul.
S.TRANSL: 當時在馬立雲的老人家，在東邊的時候還是住在一起。
At that time, the senior citizens of Maibul lived together when they were in the eastside.

S.FORM: yu pazazan tu tuyni u zazan nu silamalay kya nu ayawan cacay a lasubu izaw ku cacay a bataan a mihcanan.
S.TRANSL: 一百一十年前，開設鐵道了。
One hundred and yen years ago, the railroads were built.

S.FORM: i taway malingatu mabulaw kuyni u Maibul tayza i etip.
S.TRANSL: 馬立雲部落開始往西遷移。
Maibul tribe began to move to the west.

S.FORM: tayni i etip sa a hican.
S.TRANSL: 為何要往西?
Why (did they) go west?

S.FORM: iza kuyza u nanum nu sauwac.
S.TRANSL: 河有水。
There was water in river.

S.FORM: a kapah misalaku isa.
S.TRANSL: 所以適合耕作水田。
So it was suitable for paddy field.

S.FORM: nu amisanan sa u alangahay.
S.TRANSL: 北邊是河。
The north was the river.

S.FORM: melaw han sa yadah ku ili yadah kuyza kahmid.
S.TRANSL: 看到很多溪魚和螃蟹。
(We) saw many river fish and crabs.

S.FORM: saasu’ay nu kan kuyza u nu Pangcah.
S.TRANSL: 那些是阿美族中最好吃的食物。
Those were the most delicious foods in Amis.

S.FORM: u nu sakizaya kuyni u kalang u kahmid isaw a tuyni u ili.
S.TRANSL: 撒奇萊雅的也是螃蟹和溪魚。
It is crabs and river fish that were delicious in Sakizaya.

S.FORM: nu amisanan nu alangahay sa u buklal mamin i tiza.
S.TRANSL: 河的北邊都是河。
The north side of a river was all rivers.

S.FORM: mikacaw i katalalan sa mamelaw tu kuyni u ngabulngabul i tawya u aadupen.
S.TRANSL: 當時騎在牛上都可以看到鹿這樣的動物。
At that time, when we rode on a cow, we could see animals like deer.

S.FORM: u mahizaay kiza sak maenep kuyni i tini i Maibul ku i tiza i kasasauac.
S.TRANSL: 所以河流在馬立雲會合。
Therefore the rivers converged in Maibul.

S.FORM: nu amisanan nu kasasauac ma mahiza u sankakusin kuyza uya paenengan.
S.TRANSL: 在河流會合的北邊是一個三角形的居住地。
The north side where the river converged was a triangle place for living.

S.FORM: a enengan nu Maibul uyu paluma’an i han nu maku kuyu u kamu sa.
S.TRANSL: 我要說的是那是馬立雲部落蓋房子居住的地方。
What I would say was the place where the Maibul trible built up their houses.

S.FORM: yu mahiza sa tu sa han sa uyni u babalaki paluma tu tubah paluma tuyni u kucyan paluma tuyni u hapay tuyni u tali.
S.TRANSL: 那時候老人種地瓜、小米和芋頭。
At that time, the senior citizens grew sweet potatoes, millets and taros.

S.FORM: uyni u sakauzip yu mahiza henay mikilim tu misalaku i mahiza.
S.TRANSL: 當時找開墾水田的地方來過日子。
At that time, (we) were looking for paddy field where we could make a living.

S.FORM: i tatanga’ uyni han tu yu tatayna sa matitineng aca uyu tatayna nuyu Sakizaya.
S.TRANSL: 撒奇萊雅的女人也很會做（以下的事情）。
Sakizaya female would also do (the following things).

S.FORM: milami’ tuyza u dungec milami’ tuyni u u lukut pacamucamul han tuyni u lukut.
S.TRANSL: （女性）煮藤心再和山蘇一起煮。
(Women) cooked vine and mixed it with bird’s nest fern.

S.FORM: a tuyni u kalang atu dadipis mikilim i tiza i sauwacay asu’ ku kan mahiza kiza.
S.TRANSL: 還有在河邊找螃蟹和蟑螂，那很好吃。
And they were looking for crabs and cockroaches by the river. Those were delicious.

S.FORM: uyza han tu yu tatama sa.
S.TRANSL: 那男人呢?
How about the man?

S.FORM: tayni i buyu’ mahiza talaayaw a misanga’ tuyni u luma’ nu kilang a sizumaay henay kuyza u luma’ isa.
S.TRANSL: 來山上就這樣先去用木頭隨便蓋一個房子。
(They) came to mountain and then built a wooden house at will.

S.FORM: tatenga’ u eli’ kuyu sapadahung.
S.TRANSL: 屋頂真的是茅草。
The roof was (covered by) the thatch, indeed.

S.FORM: tu na mahiza kapah tu ku nikauzip.
S.TRANSL: 之後生活有改善了。
Then life was getting better and better.

S.FORM: izaw tu kuya misaumah i tini i hiya i Sapad.
S.TRANSL: 在掃把頂種田。
(They) farmed in Sapad.

S.FORM: i tatenga’ paluma tuyni u kucyan mahizaay kiza.
S.TRANSL: 就這樣種旱稻。
Then (they) grew rice.

S.FORM: na mahini nu ayzaay kya haw ku nikauzip nikauzip i tawya sa.
S.TRANSL: 當時的生活哪裡有像現在這樣。
Life in the past was not like that in nowadays.

S.FORM: nayi’ kuyza u hiya kwaysow satu u nu patelaw.
S.TRANSL: 當時沒有怪手，都是用人力。
There was no excavator then, only labor power.

S.FORM: uyni u tatak u cwakut u kuya u sapibangat tuyu u kilang uyni u ba’tu saka.
S.TRANSL: 把樹連根拔起，還挖出石頭。
(They) eradicated the tree and dug out the stone.

S.FORM: ahbalay sa kuyu nikalukaluk nuyza u pikilumaluma’ nuyza u babalaki mahiza henay sa.
S.TRANSL: 在當時每一戶人家種的田很寬。
The farmlands on which every family worked were wide at that time.

S.FORM: mahiza satu misimsim kuyza u babalaki a patizeng tuyni u kapahay a luma’ sa i tawya henay.
S.TRANSL: 當時老人想到要蓋好的房子。
At that time, the senior citizens thought that (they) would build good houses.

S.FORM: mikilang tuyni u hiya u tabatabakiay a kilang.
S.TRANSL: 砍很大很大的樹。
(They would) chop very big trees.

S.FORM: i milimek tu tuyni u tailing.
S.TRANSL: （他們）就躲警察。
They hided from policemen.

S.FORM: cay pamelaw tu tailing i mahiza henay.
S.TRANSL: 在當時不被警察看到。
(He) doesn't want to be seen by the policemen at that time.

S.FORM: nayay kuyu mangabilay nayay kuyu misakamuay tu cimacimanan sa.
S.TRANSL: 沒有人多嘴也不會告訴任何一個人。
No one was a big mouth and did not tell anyone.

S.FORM: kaliyuhanan kuyni u nipikilang mahiza i tini i tip.
S.TRANSL: 沒人知道在西邊砍樹。
No one knew that they chopped tree in the west side.

S.FORM: i tini i tip sa sa tu u tabakiay a kilang.
S.TRANSL: 西邊的都是大樹。
In the west side were the big trees.

S.FORM: i tiza i siutucay han nu maibul kuyza a buyu’.
S.TRANSL: 那裡有尖尖的地方就是馬立雲的山。
The place with acute shape was the mountains of Maibul.

S.FORM: tatagna’ yadah tu kuyni u kilang.
S.TRANSL: 真的有很多樹。
There were many trees.

S.FORM: E yadah tu kuyni u kilang aku isa atu kita isa.
S.TRANSL: 所以我們（包含我）已經砍了很多樹了。
So, we, including me, chopped many trees.

S.FORM: mikilim tu malucabay lalima cabay.
S.TRANSL: 找五位朋友。
(They) asked five friends.

S.FORM: misulul tuyu u kilang.
S.TRANSL: 讓樹滑下來。
(They) glided the tree.

S.FORM: misulul tuyu u kilang sa kanca u tatayna mamin tu pahalhal tuyni u hiya tu malukakanen.
S.TRANSL: 在(男人)運木頭的時候，女人一定準備好食物。
When (men were) transporting logs, women had to prepare foods.

S.FORM: maduh tuyni u panay anu u habay mahiza sa misatinuz.
S.TRANSL: 摏糯米或小米來做麻糬。
(Women) mashed glutinous rice or millet to make rice cakes.

S.FORM: anu misulul tuya u kilang mahizaay kiza ku nika ku tineng nuyza u acawa nu niyam u Sakizaya.
S.TRANSL: (男人)在運木頭的時候，撒奇萊雅的婦女都這麼能幹。
When (men) were transporting logs, Sakizaya women were so competent.

S.FORM: anu misulul kuyu tatama tuyni u kilang han nu maku.
S.TRANSL: 我要說的是男人運木頭。
What I would say was that men transported logs.

S.FORM: sasimutizengay sa nuyni u hiya nu luma’ tuyni u tika u tika hananay sa.
S.TRANSL: 有站著的，那是房子且叫做Tika。
There were some which stood; they were houses and called “Tika”.

S.FORM: anu lokusenkaku anu nanasenkaku badahung sa uyni u kawala kuyu u badahung.
S.TRANSL: 屋頂用瓦。
The roof was covered with tiles.

S.FORM: izaw ku sitenzyoay a nipisanga’ kapahay kuyza u hiya lalabu nu luma’.
S.TRANSL: 有做天花板的是家境好的。
The house that had ceiling was a good one.

S.FORM: nu tenzyo nayay kaku an sa sa cay kapatenzyo.
S.TRANSL: 如果我沒有（錢）的話，就不做天花板。
If I had no (money), I would not have ceiling.

S.FORM: kawala kuyza u luma’ u secubi uini pisanga’ tuyni u kakabi’an sa uyni u hinuki.
S.TRANSL: 設備有像是用檜木做的床。
(There were) accommodation like a bed made of Japanese cypress.

S.FORM: mahizaay kuyza kuyu nu si kalisiway.
S.TRANSL: 這樣就是有錢人。
That were real people.

S.FORM: ya han nu maku kuyni u kamu.
S.TRANSL: 那就是我要說的。
That was what I would say.

S.FORM: sa tu sa u yu lipun yu mahiza izaw tu kuyni u lipun maputuh kuyni u lipun.
S.TRANSL: 當時日本人忌妒。
At that time, Japanese envied them.

S.FORM: uyza han tu uyu nikalukan nuyni nu babalaki i tini i Sapat sa alawen nuyni u lipun.
S.TRANSL: 至於老人家在掃把頂耕作的田被日本人搶走了。
The old people’s farmland in Sapat were taken away by the Japanese.

S.FORM: yu mahiza cay paacai ku babalaki.
S.TRANSL: 老人家沒有賣。
The senior citizens did not sell.

S.FORM: palumaen nu lipun tuyni u kohi mahizaay kiza ku nika uzip nu babalaki u kakadihi an kuyni u babalaki mahiza nuyni u sakizaya i han nu maku kuyni u kamu.
S.TRANSL: 我就這樣說日本人就種咖啡，老人這樣生活非常可憐。
I would say that the Japanese grew coffee so that the senior citizens lived in this way, which was very pitiful.

S.FORM: uyza u sibalucu’ayay nuyni u nu micudaday i tini i hiya i kalipunan i kalipunan sa tatenga’ izaw kya picudadan i tawya nu babalaki.
S.TRANSL: 在日治時期當時有學校給真的有心要讀書的人。
There were schools then for people who really wanted to study in Japanese ruling period of time.

S.FORM: cay pakatukuh i tini i enemay a mihcaan kuyni u cudad katukuh i sepatay a mihca sa E pahezek tu.
S.TRANSL: 不用六年，四年就完成了。
It did not take six years, but four years, to finish.

S.FORM: mibiye tu sepat a mihca hatiza mibiye.
S.TRANSL: 只有四年就畢業了。
It took four years to graduate.

S.FORM: anu ayza sa enem a mihca.
S.TRANSL: 現在是六年。
It is six years now.

S.FORM: i tawya sa koga’ko i han koga’ko han sa katukuh i sepatay a mihca sa nu enemay a mihca ku tangah.
S.TRANSL: Koga’ko就是到了四年級有六年級的頭腦。
Koga’ko means that the fourth-grade students had the brain of sixth-grade students.

S.FORM: ya han nu maku kuyza u kamu sa.
S.TRANSL: 我這樣說。
That was what I would say.

S.FORM: yu mahica henay u babalaki sibalucu’ micudad tayni i lipun.
S.TRANSL: 當時老人有心來日本讀書。
At that time, the senior citizens intended to study in Japan.

S.FORM: sakatineng tuyni u Kawas.
S.TRANSL: （他們）要認識神。
(They wanted to) know God.

S.FORM: uyza han tu sa ci baki ci Kacaw Emang.
S.TRANSL: 那就是Kacaw Emang。
That was Kacaw Emang.

S.FORM: micudad tuyni pakainiay i nu kawasan.
S.TRANSL: （他）研讀神學。
(He) studied theology.

S.FORM: u seisyo kuyni u cudaden.
S.TRANSL: 聖經就是讀的東西。
The bible was what (they) studied.

S.FORM: pakaini i Kalingku i tini i Taytu na tusaay a ken ciniza ku misalakuay.
S.TRANSL: 從花蓮及這個台東這二縣是他來耕耘。
He was the one who worked in the two counties, Hualian and Taitung.

S.FORM: patizeng tuyni u sekyokay mahizaay kiza.
S.TRANSL: （他）就這樣成立聖教會。
(He) established Holiness church.

S.FORM: kilul sa i tiniay tu i Maibul ci Kaniw Iku ci Bakah Kumud.
S.TRANSL: 馬立雲的Kaniw Iku 和Bakah Kumud就跟隨著 (Kacaw Emang 的腳步)。
Kaniw Iku and Bakah Kumud from Maibul followed (what Kacaw Emang did).

S.FORM: i na tatusaay sa tayniay i tini i lipun micudad tokyo a pakainiay i gicyucu.
S.TRANSL: 那二位到日本東京學習技術相關的。
Those two went to Tokyo, Japan and study techniques.

S.FORM: misyuli tuyni u kikay.
S.TRANSL: 修理機械的。
(It was to) fix machines.

S.FORM: u gicyucuka.
S.TRANSL: 是技術家。
(They) were technicians.

S.FORM: senka kuyu picudad.
S.TRANSL: 是專家。
(They were) specialists.

S.FORM: taluma’ sa tu sa i tini Kutubuki nu seitokaysya u sawanengan.
S.TRANSL: 回來後在壽豐鄉的糖廠。
(He) worked in the sugar factory in Shoufeng Township after he came back.

S.FORM: kuyza u pisanpan i mahiza henay mikuli.
S.TRANSL: 當時分做三班工作。
They worked in three-shift system.

S.FORM: ya han nu maku nika u kamu.
S.TRANSL: 那就是我要說的。
That was what I would say.

S.FORM: i tatulu henay u saka sasepat sa.
S.TRANSL: 第四位呢?
So the fourth one?

S.FORM: E cacay a bataan na mamin micudad i tini i Kuhkuh.
S.TRANSL: 在瑞穗念完了書。
(He) finished his studying in Rueisuei Township.

S.FORM: i tini tu i wali a cacudadan.
S.TRANSL: 還是在東部的學校。
The school was in the east.

S.FORM: maliyuh kaku u tengil nu maku sa tahkal i tini i cacudadan sa i tayni tu i lipun.
S.TRANSL: 我不是很清楚，我聽到的是畢業之後就來日本。
I did not know well and what I heard was that (he) came to Japan after graduation.

S.FORM: micudad tu cacay a bataan a mihcanan.
S.TRANSL: 讀十年書。
(He) studied for ten years.

S.FORM: cacay a bataan a mihcanan sa.
S.TRANSL: 是十年。
It is ten years.

S.FORM: saayaway niza a micudad sa na i tini tu i syogak.
S.TRANSL: 最初是唸這裡的小學。
First, (he) studied in the elementary school here.

S.FORM: mapahzek kuyu zyugak sa.
S.TRANSL: 中學畢業。
(He) graduated from junior high school.

S.FORM: i’ki ina wawa ca’ay ka u nialaan nu niyam u lipun a taini i tini i lipun a papicudad u nu cudad.
S.TRANSL: 不是日本人要錄取那小孩來日本讀書的。
It is not Japanese that admitted that child to come to Japan and study in Japan.

S.FORM: mahini ku nika tayni nu misu saayaway sa mikacaw tuyni u balunga.
S.TRANSL: 最先是搭船來的。
(He), first of all, came here by taking a ship.

S.FORM: na makaini i hiya sa i i balunga sa madebu i tini i lipun.
S.TRANSL: 就坐船到日本。
(He) took ship to Japan.

S.FORM: i tawya sa uyza uyza a hiya a basu kuyu kacawen kya.
S.TRANSL: 當時可能是搭公車。
He might take bus at that time.

S.FORM: a katukuh i tini i hiya i luma’ isa.
S.TRANSL: 就到家了。
Then (he) got home.

S.FORM: i tatenga’ izaw kuyza u nu paliayaway a cudad a pasubana’ cay ka u nikelidan nu tamdaw.
S.TRANSL: 沒有人教他，帶領他。
No one taught him and led him.

S.FORM: ina wawa tuyni yu na babalaki haw sa.
S.TRANSL: 那小孩就是那老人家 (第四位)。
That child was that senior citizen (the fourth one).

S.FORM: tadamatineng kuyu u tangah niza mimelaw tuyu u cudad sa.
S.TRANSL: 他知道很多，很會讀書。
He knew very a lot and studied well.

S.FORM: katukuh sa i tini i lipun de i tini i lipun sa micudad henay i zyugak.
S.TRANSL: 到達日本之後，後再念中學。
After arriving in Japan, (he) studied in junior high school.

S.FORM: mapahzek kuyu zyugak sa.
S.TRANSL: 國中畢業。
(He) graduated from junior high school.

S.FORM: i minanam ciniza ku nika micudad tuyni u hiya.
S.TRANSL: 他讀書練習。
He studied and practiced.

S.FORM: anu minamam han sa minamam tu.
S.TRANSL: 叫(他)練習他就練習。
(He) would practice if being asked.

S.FORM: i miasip tuyni u pakainiay i a asipen tuyni u hiya u nu bunsang.
S.TRANSL: 他念有關和尚的。
(He) studied (the subject) of monks.

S.FORM: uyza han tuyza sa tezay u nipihiya miasip kuyza.
S.TRANSL: （他）留在那裡念。
(He) stayed there and studied.

S.FORM: ya han nu maku ku kamu.
S.TRANSL: 這就是我要說的。
This was what I would say.

S.FORM: u nika i tiza i lipun niza babalaki sa ni Adup Pucu’ sa cacay a bataan a mihcanan.
S.TRANSL: 在日本的那個老人家Adup Pucu’（待了）十年。
The senior citizen, Adup Pucu’, (stayed) in Japan for ten years.

S.FORM: taluma’ sa tu ciniza sa i tatenga’ kalipunan henay kuyni.
S.TRANSL: 他回來時真的還是日治時代。
It was still Japanese ruling period when he came back.

S.FORM: E bunsang nu i tiniay i kalingku i tini i Taytu mahizaay ku nikauzip ni Adup Pucu’ kiza.
S.TRANSL: （他是）在花蓮在台東的和尚，他的生活就是這樣。
(He was) the monk in Huanlian and Taitung, and his life was in that way.

S.FORM: ya han nu maku ku kamu.
S.TRANSL: 這就是我要說的。
This was what I would say.

S.FORM: sa zikuz nuyni u hiya sa nu ni Adup Pucu’ sa i na izaw tu ku mikilulay a micudad.
S.TRANSL: 在Adup Pucu’之後，隨著他（的腳步去）讀書。
After Adup Pucu’, (they) followed him to study.

S.FORM: ci Kuli Kaniw ya han micudad i tini i wasita daigaku.
S.TRANSL: Kuli Kaniw 在早稻田大學讀書。
Kuli Kaniw studied at Waseda University.

S.FORM: ciniza sa tatenga’ sa mapahzek ku nipicudad i tini i wasita daigaku sa.
S.TRANSL: 他真的從早稻田大學畢業。
He did graduate from Waseda University.

S.FORM: E taluma’ tu taluma’ ciniza sa malasinsi nu picudadan.
S.TRANSL: 回來後，成為學校的老師。
After coming back, he became school teacher.

S.FORM: mahiza kiza u nika u lencay nu Maibul.
S.TRANSL: 是馬立雲的人才。
(He) was a talent of Maibul.

S.FORM: u lencay nu Maibul cay kuyni a dada.
S.TRANSL: 馬立雲的人才不只這些。
There were more talents of Maibul than these.

S.FORM: izaw kuyza malataylingay.
S.TRANSL: 也有人當警察。
There was someone who became a policeman.

S.FORM: yu mahiza henay izaw kuyza malasinsiay.
S.TRANSL: 仍然還是也有成為老師的人。
There was still someone who became a teacher.

S.FORM: ya han nu maku kuyni u kamu.
S.TRANSL: 這就是我要說的。
That was what I would say.

S.FORM: ak adidi kuya u hiya u niyazu’ ya han tu.
S.TRANSL: 部落很小。
The tribe was small.

S.FORM: ya han tu sa ya uyza sa u balucu’ nu niyam u Sakizaya u babalaki sa isaw.
S.TRANSL: 那是撒奇萊雅老人的心啦。
That was the heart of Sakizaya senior citizens.

S.FORM: icelang a misabalucu’ tuyni u pakainiay i sakauzip.
S.TRANSL: 下定決心努力地做有關生計的事。
(They) determined to work diligently to make a living.

S.FORM: ya han nu maku kuyni u kamu sa.
S.TRANSL: 這就是我要說的。
This was what I would say.

S.FORM: ya uyni han tu u hiya u babalaki yu mahiza henay matineng miadup tuyni u a adupen i tini i etip.
S.TRANSL: 有關這個，老人家當時在西邊很會打獵。
Speaking of this, the senior citizens were good at hunting in the west.

S.FORM: siutucay.
S.TRANSL: （那是）有尖形的（地方）。
(That was a place) that had the sharp shape.

S.FORM: u sakauzip nu babalaki.
S.TRANSL: 老人家生存的（方法）。
(The way) the senior citizens made a living.

S.FORM: kuyni u niadupan i tawya u sizi u ngabul.
S.TRANSL: 當時獵羊和鹿。
(They) hunted goats and deer at that time.

S.FORM: u mahizaay kiza sa.
S.TRANSL: 就這樣。
That’s it.

S.FORM: uyni han tu u alangahay han sa izaw ku sakacacay sakatusa sakatulu nuyni u alangahayay i tini i hiza na makaizaay i hiya.
S.TRANSL: 有第一條第二條第三條的河。
There were the first, the second and the third rivers.

S.FORM: i Kuyuwan han Kuyuwan han i tawya nu babalaki.
S.TRANSL: 當時老人家稱做Kuyuwan。
At that time, the senior citizens called it “Kuyuwan.”

S.FORM: ayzaay a kamu kya koyoson hongye han ayza.
S.TRANSL: 現在叫做紅葉。
It is called “Hongye village.”

S.FORM: kuyni u kamu tu hiya tu pakainiay i Kuyuwan han.
S.TRANSL: 這就是講關於Kuyuwan。
That words were about Kuyuwan.

S.FORM: u sihiyaay u sinilaculay u izaway mamin kuyni hiya nya alangahay tuyza u kahmid ya kahmid hananay sa.
S.TRANSL: 這些河都有毛蟹。
The rivers had crabs.

S.FORM: u sumanahay ku luma’ nya kahmid u uzip sa.
S.TRANSL: 螃蟹的殼是紅色的。
The shell of crab was red.

S.FORM: asu’ kuyu u kabi u heci sa asu’.
S.TRANSL: 湯好喝，肉好吃。
The soup and the flesh were delicious.

S.FORM: u ili yadah.
S.TRANSL: 溪魚很多。
There were a lot of river fish.

S.FORM: mahizaay kiza sa.
S.TRANSL: 就這樣。
That’s it.

S.FORM: na enel sa kuyni u Maibul manamuh aca.
S.TRANSL: 馬立雲部落的人也很喜歡，捨不得走。
People of Maibul liked it and were unwilling to go.

S.FORM: a midadingu tuyni u hiya micucuk.
S.TRANSL: 戴蛙鏡，再來刺魚。
(They) put on goggles and speared fish.

S.FORM: izaw kuyza u sacucuk nuyza u tatayna midadingu nisanga’an nuyza u hiya tatama tuyza malusapidadingu tuyni nu hiya.
S.TRANSL: 女人刺魚，有男人做的眼鏡。
Famale speared (fish) and had the goggles which were made by male.

S.FORM: tuyni u u ili mahizaay kiza.
S.TRANSL: 就是這樣（刺）溪魚。
(They speared) river fish in this way.

S.FORM: anu u amisan nu alangahay han sa uyni u hiya u bukelal mamin na makaluk kya haw kuyza u bukelal yu mahiza henay.
S.TRANSL: 在河的北邊，當時河岸怎麼能耕種?
In the north side of the river, how could they plant by the riverside?

S.FORM: u katalalan sa iza ku katalalan nu hiya nu Maibul.
S.TRANSL: 馬立雲有牛。
There were cows in Maibul.

S.FORM: malukay aca isaw sa.
S.TRANSL: 是因為種田的啦。
That was due to farming.

S.FORM: pana’en aca kuwangen aca maala kuyza u hiya u aadupen yu mahiza henay sa.
S.TRANSL: 用箭射的、用槍射的，就可以射到動物。
(They) shot with arrows or with gun so that they could hunt some animals.

S.FORM: i tatanga’ dada ka pah ku nikauzip yu mahiza henay nu yu babalaki.
S.TRANSL: 老人當時生活很好。
The senior citizens’s life at that time was very good.

S.FORM: sa kina katineng nu maku.
S.TRANSL: 這是我所知道的。
This was what I knew.

S.FORM: ya han nu maku kuyza u kamu sa.
S.TRANSL: 這就是我要說的。
This was what I said.

S.FORM: anu ayza tu ku nikauzip u nu sazikuzikuzay tu nu ayzaay tu nu kahulamanan.
S.TRANSL: 若是現在的生活，也就是最後的、現在的民國。
The life nowadays, the lattest, is Minkuo.

S.FORM: ya ahican saw.
S.TRANSL: 那怎樣呢?
How about it?

S.FORM: anu sayayaway nuyza u hulam sa.
S.TRANSL: 最初的是外省人。
In the early time were the mainlanders.

S.FORM: i tatanga’ anu sapaw tu pi tu pina anu muktep anu sapaw tu pina a mihcanan anu katukuhanan tu ku nika izaw tu kuni sausi nu papaw nuyni u kensehu sa.
S.TRANSL: 十幾年縣政府有打算。
In a decade, the county government was making plans.

S.FORM: uyni han tu u nisata’anan sa.
S.TRANSL: 所交的稅。
The tax they paid.

S.FORM: tanengay hongyaen sa hongya tu.
S.TRANSL: 可以申請的，就申請。
(They) could apply if they could.

S.FORM: anu cacay a bataan a mihcanan ku nipihongya tu lala sa tahkal tu kuyza u tingki.
S.TRANSL: 如果申請土地權狀，十年以後登記的就會出來了。
If (you) applied for the land ownership certificate.

S.FORM: mahizaay kiza nu nu kaliumahan nuyni u Sakizaya ku nu amisan nu alangahay sa.
S.TRANSL: 河的北邊就是撒奇萊雅的耕地。
The north side was the farmland of Sakizaya.

S.FORM: E maalaw nuyni u lipun izaw tu ku lipun sa.
S.TRANSL: （耕地）在日治時代被日本人搶了。
(We) were robbed of the farmland by Japanese in Japanese Ruling Period.

S.FORM: tuyni papalumaan tuyni u tubah papalumaan tuyni u kubkub tuudtuud u sakauzip mahizaay kiza sa.
S.TRANSL: 所種植的地瓜與玉蜀黍是生活所必需的。
The sweet yam and maize were the necessities of life.

S.FORM: na zikuzan nu lipun sa i u Taiwan tu.
S.TRANSL: 日本人之後，是閩南人。
After Japanese was the Southern Min.

S.FORM: u micakacakay tu yu na nu lipun a a umah.
S.TRANSL: 閩南人買日本人的地。
The Southern Min people bought the land belonging to Japanese.

S.FORM: malainayay tu kuyni u sakauzip tuyni u Sakizaya i tiniay i Maibul.
S.TRANSL: 在馬立雲的撒奇萊雅人就沒辦法生活。
The Sakzaya people in Maibul could not make a living.

S.FORM: iza sa izaw henay kuyza malukay i tini nu amisan nu alangahay.
S.TRANSL: 還有人在河的北邊種田。
There were some people who planted in the north side of the river.

S.FORM: misinsi kami u Sakizaya sa tatanga’ E sisabu tu pina a mihcanan ku nika u pawliwdi sa.
S.TRANSL: 撒奇萊雅人申請保留地十幾年。
Sakizaya people applied for the reserved region for more than ten years.

S.FORM: pawliwdi han sa kakatukuh i mahini sa ca’ay henay ka tahkal kuyni u tingki.
S.TRANSL: 保留地呢，到現在登記了還沒出來。
Speaking of the reserved region, there came no result until now.

S.FORM: mahicaay ku nipihiya.
S.TRANSL: 不知做的如何?
How did they that?

S.FORM: nipikuwan nuyni u kEnsEhu.
S.TRANSL: 縣政府管的。
(This was) managed by the county government.

S.FORM: izaw ku nipisimsim mabialaw tu isaw.
S.TRANSL: 我們還是很緊張。
We are nervous now.

S.FORM: cay tu kauyzauyza u yu asali.
S.TRANSL: 不那麼乾脆。
(They) were not so frank.

S.FORM: a balucu’ nu nitikuan nuyza u lipun nitikuan nu na zukuzan nu lipun.
S.TRANSL: 日本人之後在重做。
(They) did it again after Japaense.

S.FORM: sasayaway nuyni u hiya nu yakuba nuyni u kensehu sa ka pah kuni pikuwan tu pakainiay i malukay sa.
S.TRANSL: 最先是鄉公所，然後是縣政府，農業管理的很好。
It was the township government in the very beginning and then the county government that managed argriculture well.

S.FORM: ayza haw i uyni han tu u Maibul sa.
S.TRANSL: 現在馬立雲呢?
How about Maibul now?

S.FORM: sina kalahan tu.
S.TRANSL: They were nervous.
他們很緊張。

S.FORM: milunguc tuyni u sakatahkal nuyni u cudad nuyza u nikalukan.
S.TRANSL: 他們要求（政府）把土地權狀做出來。
They asked (the government) to hand in the land ownership certificate.

S.FORM: mahizaay kiza sa.
S.TRANSL: 就這樣。
That’s it!

S.FORM: cay tu kauyzauyza kuyza u nu ayzaay a nikauzip.
S.TRANSL: 現在的生活已經不再是那樣。
Nowadays, life is not in that way.

S.FORM: tahkal tu mikuli.
S.TRANSL: 出去工作。
They went out to work.

S.FORM: an cay pikuli sa nayay ku yu sapahapay tuyni u wawa.
S.TRANSL: 如果不工作，就無法養小孩。
If (we) did not work, (we) could not raise children.

S.FORM: nayay kuyni u cacay i tini i pabaw.
S.TRANSL: 沒有工作。
(We) did not work.

S.FORM: sananay kyo sa saicelang sa kuyni u ayzaay nikauzip.
S.TRANSL: 因為這樣，所以現在要生活就要很努力。
Owing to this, now (we) have to work diligently to make a living.

S.FORM: i tawyaay a babalaki sa i nayay tu amin mabelec tu.
S.TRANSL: 以前的老人家沒有回家。
The senior citizens in the past did not go home.

S.FORM: u nunui katengilan aku tuyni u kamu u kamu nuyni u pakunkuay saka.
S.TRANSL: 這個我聽到的是那個講故事的人說的。
This is what heard from the storyteller.

S.FORM: matineng kamu tuyni u pakainiay i Maibul.
S.TRANSL: 我知道關於馬立雲的事。
I knew the story about Maibul.

S.FORM: ya han han nu maku ku nika u kamu hatizaay kina kamu nu maku.
S.TRANSL: 我的話到此為止。
This is what I would say.
